name: Khmelnytskyi Oblast (Ukraine)

colors.symbolism: the sky and the historic Podolian sun on its azure field
the historic banner of the Cossack Bohdan Khmelnytsky (after whom the oblast is named)

description: A vertical bicolour — blue at the hoist and red at the fly (the blue field twice as wide as the red), with the coat of arms of Khmelnytskyi Oblast charged on the blue.
history: Approved by the regional council on 21 March 2002 during its twenty-second session. Khmelnytskyi Oblast covers most of historic Podolia and is named after the Cossack Hetman Bohdan Khmelnytsky.

features.role: arms of Khmelnytskyi Oblast — Podolian sun
